name: Fabero and Alto Sil Loop
name_original: Fabero desde Toreno – circular por los pueblos del Sil – Espacio Natural de los Ancares Leoneses y Alto Sil
description: This moderate 34.5-mile (55.4 km) road cycling route takes you through the scenic Natural Space of Los Ancares Leoneses and Alto Sil.
region_intro: Road cycling around Toreno features diverse terrain, including routes with significant elevation changes. The area offers a network of paths suitable for various road cycling experiences. Cyclists can expect a mix of ascents and descents, providing varied challenges across the landscape. Best road cycling routes around Toreno The most popular road cycling route is Fabero and Alto Sil Loop, a 34.4 miles (55.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes over 2,600 feet (800 m) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ponferrada Castle – View of Bembibre loop from aparcamiento municipal, a moderate 33.9 miles (54.6 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding area with over 2,000 feet (600 m) of climbing. Local road cyclists also love the Toreno – Puerto de Cerredo loop from Toreno, a 55.9 miles (89.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 33 minutes. This difficult route features over 4,300 feet (1,300 m) of elevation gain. Road cycling around Toreno is defined by routes with notable elevation changes, offering both moderate and difficult challenges. The network provides options for different ability levels, from shorter loops to longer, more demanding rides. The routes in Toreno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Toreno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Toreno?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes around Toreno, offering a wide range of experiences from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The komoot community has explored these routes over 1,300 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Toreno?
A: Road cycling around Toreno features diverse terrain with notable elevation changes. You'll find a mix of ascents and descents, providing varied challenges. Routes range from moderate, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042325321/fabero-and-alto-sil-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fabero and Alto Sil Loop&lt;/a&gt; with over 2,600 feet (800 m) of gain, to difficult, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toreno – Puerto de Cerredo loop from Toreno&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 4,300 feet (1,300 m) of climbing.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Toreno?
A: Yes, Toreno offers a few easier road cycling options. While many routes involve significant elevation, there are 3 routes classified as easy, providing a gentler introduction to the area's cycling. These are ideal for those looking for less strenuous rides.

Q: What natural features can I see along the road cycling routes in Toreno?
A: As you cycle around Toreno, you can encounter various natural features. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Catoute (2,117 m)&lt;/a&gt; summit, and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zofreral de Arriba forest&lt;/a&gt;. These offer scenic breaks and beautiful vistas during your ride.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4838103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Esteban de Columbrianos&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6183733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Dam on the Sil River&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Vega de Espinareda&lt;/a&gt;. These add a cultural dimension to your cycling experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Toreno?
A: The road cycling routes in Toreno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which offers both moderate and difficult challenges, and the scenic beauty of the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Toreno?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Toreno are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponferrada Castle – View of Bembibre loop from aparcamiento municipal&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igüeña Recreation Area loop from aparcamiento municipal&lt;/a&gt; are both circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Toreno?
A: Given the region's diverse terrain and potential for elevation, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Toreno, with milder temperatures. Summer can also be suitable, especially for higher elevation routes, but be prepared for warmer weather in lower areas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Toreno?
A: While many routes in Toreno feature significant climbs, there are easier options that might be more suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. Look for routes classified as 'easy' to find paths with gentler gradients and shorter distances.

Q: Where can I find parking facilities for cyclists near the routes in Toreno?
A: Many routes around Toreno, especially those starting from towns or recreational areas, often have designated parking facilities. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponferrada Castle – View of Bembibre loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igüeña Recreation Area loop&lt;/a&gt; explicitly mention starting from municipal parking areas, making it convenient for cyclists arriving by car.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Toreno?
A: Road cycling routes are primarily designed for bikes on paved surfaces. While some areas may have adjacent paths where dogs are permitted, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs on road cycling routes themselves due to safety concerns with traffic and speed. Always check local regulations for specific trails or parks if you plan to cycle with a dog.
tips: Matarrosa del Sil is a small mining town on the banks of the River Sil.
This coal-fired power plant and its cooling tower and lagoon are located in the heart of the Ancares Leoneses and Alto Sil Natural Area.
An industrial mining town surrounded by mountain peaks near the Espacio Natural de los Ancares Leoneses y Alto Sil.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route is approximately 55.4 kilometers long. Cyclists with good fitness levels can expect to complete it in about 2 hours and 40 minutes.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate road ride. While the surfaces are generally well-paved, it includes an elevation gain of about 800 meters. Good fitness is recommended, so it might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect predominantly well-paved roads that are easy to navigate. The route is designed for road bikes, offering good conditions throughout, despite being in a natural area that can sometimes feature unpaved sections on other trails.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll pass by several notable spots. These include the Church of Matarrosa del Sil, the Anllares del Sil Thermal Power Plant, and a scenic View of Fabero.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I see while cycling?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the Natural Space of Los Ancares Leoneses and Alto Sil. You'll experience scenic landscapes characteristic of a protected natural area, including views of valleys, mountains, and picturesque traditional villages of Sil, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural elements.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will encounter parts of the Camino Natural La Mirada Circular, Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, [CIMA LE16] Calello - Corral de los Lobos, Camino de Vega de Espinareda, Camino de la Pradera, and Pasarela de Ángel Velasco.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Since this is a circular route starting and ending in Toreno via Fabero, you can typically find parking in or near the villages of Toreno or Fabero to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is on public roads, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs on a road cycling trip of this distance and difficulty, especially given the potential for traffic and the physical demands on a pet. For walking trails in the region, leash rules usually apply.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the best weather and scenery, the ideal time to cycle in the Natural Space of Los Ancares Leoneses and Alto Sil is typically during the spring, early summer, or autumn months. These periods offer milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Ancares Leoneses?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Ancares Leoneses. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas, and stay on designated routes.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through several villages of Sil, including Fabero and Toreno, you will likely find opportunities for refreshments, cafes, or restaurants in these towns. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially for longer stretches between villages.
